item.title: Issue #2674 created
item.description:
Editorial notes (to be continued)

A sequel, starting with Gunther’s observation in the last comment of #2590:

  • [ ] XQuery: A.1.2 Constraint: reserved-function-names uses the term "unprefixed" as one of the characteristics of disallowed function names. This may lead one to believe that a URIQualifiedName without a prefix might possibly qualify for being restricted. However, this seems unlikely to be intended: the Q{...}... syntax is unambiguous and cannot be mistaken for a KindTest or similar construct. I thus propose to clarify this in the constraint, by dropping "prefixed"/"unprefixed" from it, or adding a remark about URIQualifiedName.
